definition: Formal regime-switching models (Markov-switching GARCH) are overfitted and suffer from fundamental identification lag — smoothed probabilities require 10-20 trading days to reach >80% confidence, transition parameters are estimated from non-representative samples, and within-regime dynamics have changed due to microstructure evolution. Simple threshold heuristics (e.g., MOVE at 80, 100, 140) remain operationally useful as real-time observable alternatives.
evidence: Smoothed probabilities require 10-20 trading days to reach >80% confidence
Transition parameters estimated from non-representative samples
Within-regime dynamics changed due to microstructure evolution
Well-documented in academic literature (Ang and Timmermann 2012)
Simple MOVE thresholds provide real-time regime classification without identification lag
open_questions: Can hybrid approaches (simple thresholds plus Bayesian updating) outperform either pure approach?
What are the optimal threshold levels for the current structural environment?
How should models account for microstructure evolution within regimes?
